title: claude-fleet fanout manifest
description: A partition of the repository into PAIRWISE DISJOINT units, one headless worker per unit, each in its own worktree. `fleet delegate fanout <manifest>` PROVES the partition disjoint (via check-claims.py) before it launches anything, and REFUSES (exit 2) if it is not. Disjointness is a precondition, not advice: Anthropic ran 16 agents at one task and 'every agent would hit the same bug, fix that bug, and then overwrite each other's changes' — N agents on overlapping work is strictly worse than N=1, so no --jobs value can rescue a bad manifest.

id.description: Unique. Becomes a branch (agent/fanout/<manifest-slug>/<id>) and a state directory.

owns.description: The unit's file partition, as repo-relative globs (no leading '/', no '..'). A bare directory means its whole subtree and is normalised to '<dir>/**'. MUST be pairwise disjoint with every other unit's `owns` — proven by check-claims.py, which also rejects globs that target the repo's `forbidden` (generated) paths.

task.description: The unit's brief. Handed to a headless `claude -p` worker running in that unit's worktree, together with the `owns` set and an instruction never to write outside it.

branch.description: Optional. Override the derived branch name. Must satisfy the repo's branchPattern (see .claude/agent-claims.json).
